lines.speaker: Town crier
lines.text: Hello citizen!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you do around here?

lines.speaker: Town crier
lines.text: I'm a Town Crier. It's my job to let people know of any recent news. After all, there's all sorts of things happening around here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see. See you later.

lines.speaker: Town crier
lines.text: Until next time.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: See you later.

lines.speaker: Town crier
lines.text: Until next time.

lines.speaker: Town crier
lines.text: Hear ye! Hear ye! Player Moderators massive help to RuneSc-

lines.speaker: Town crier
lines.text: Oh, hello citizen. Are you here to find out about Player Moderators? Or perhaps would you like to know about the laws of the land?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about Player Moderators.

lines.speaker: Town crier
lines.text: Of course. What would you like to know?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What is a Player Moderator?

lines.speaker: Town crier
lines.text: Player Moderators are normal players of the game, just like you. However, since they have shown themselves to be trustworthy and active reporters, they have been invited by Jagex to monitor the game and

lines.speaker: Town crier
lines.text: take appropriate action when they see rule breaking. You can spot a Player Moderator in game by looking at the chat screen - when a Player Moderator speaks, a silver crown appears to the left of their name.

lines.speaker: Town crier
lines.text: Remember, if there's not silver crown there, they are not a Player Moderator!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks!

lines.speaker: Town crier
lines.text: Is there anything else you'd like to know?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What can Player Moderators do?

lines.speaker: Town crier
lines.text: Player Moderators, or 'P-mods', have the ability to mute rule breakers and Jagex view their reports as a priority so that action is taken as quickly as possible. P- Mods also have access to the Player Moderator Centre.

lines.speaker: Town crier
lines.text: Within the Centre are tools to help them Moderate Old School RuneScape. These tools include dedicated forums, the Player Moderator Guidelines and the Player Moderator Code of Conduct.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks!

lines.speaker: Town crier
lines.text: Is there anything else you'd like to know?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do I become a Player Moderator?

lines.speaker: Town crier
lines.text: Jagex picks players who spend their time and effort to help better the Old School RuneScape community. To increase your chances of becoming a Player Moderator:

lines.speaker: Town crier
lines.text: Keep your account secure! This is very important, as a player with poor security will never be a P-Mod. Read our Security Tips for more information.

lines.speaker: Town crier
lines.text: Play by the rules! The rules of Old School RuneScape are enforced for a reason, to make the game a fair and enjoyable environment for all.

lines.speaker: Town crier
lines.text: Report accurately! When Jagex consider an account for review they look for quality, not quantity. Ensure your reports are of a high quality by following the report guidelines.

lines.speaker: Town crier
lines.text: Be excellent to each other! Treat others as you would want to be treated yourself. Respect your fellow player.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks!

lines.speaker: Town crier
lines.text: Is there anything else you'd like to know?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What can Player Moderators not do?

lines.speaker: Town crier
lines.text: P-Mods cannot ban your account - they can only report offences. Jagex then take action based on the evidence received. If you lose your password or get scammed by another player, P-Mods cannot help you

lines.speaker: Town crier
lines.text: get your account back. All they can do is recommend you to go to Customer Support. They cannot retrieve any items you may have lost and receive no free items from Jagex for helping moderate the game. They are

lines.speaker: Town crier
lines.text: players who give their efforts to help the community out of the goodness of their hearts! P-mods do not work for Jagex and so cannot make you a Moderator, or recommend other accounts to become moderators. If

lines.speaker: Town crier
lines.text: you wish to become a Moderator, please feel free to ask me how!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks!

lines.speaker: Town crier
lines.text: Is there anything else you'd like to know?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about the Rules of Conduct.

lines.speaker: Town crier
lines.text: At once. Take a look at my book here...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you give me a handy tip please?

lines.speaker: Town crier
lines.text: Did you know most skills have right click 'Make-X' options to help you train faster?

lines.speaker: Town crier
lines.text: Make your recovery questions and answers hard to guess but easy to remember.

lines.speaker: Town crier
lines.text: Don't use your RuneScape password on other sites. Keep your account safe!

lines.speaker: Town crier
lines.text: If you think someone knows your password - change it!

lines.speaker: Town crier
lines.text: The squirrels! The squirrels are coming! Noooo, get them out of my head!

lines.speaker: Town crier
lines.text: Did you know you burn food less often on the range in Lumbridge castle than other ranges?

lines.speaker: Town crier
lines.text: Players can not trim armour. Don't fall for this popular scam!

lines.speaker: Town crier
lines.text: Feeling harassed? Don't forget your ignore list can be especially useful if a player seems to be picking on you!

lines.speaker: Town crier
lines.text: If you're lost and have no idea where to go, use the Home Teleport spell for free!

lines.speaker: Town crier
lines.text: If you see someone breaking the rules, report them!

lines.speaker: Town crier
lines.text: Did you know? Superheat Item means you never fail to smelt ore!

lines.speaker: Town crier
lines.text: Did you know that at high levels of Runecrafting you get more than one rune per essence?

lines.speaker: Town crier
lines.text: Never lend your items out unless you expect them not to come back.

lines.speaker: Town crier
lines.text: Never question a penguin.

lines.speaker: Town crier
lines.text: Melee armour actually gives you disadvantages when using magic attacks. It may be better to take off your armour entirely!

lines.speaker: Town crier
lines.text: If the chat window is moving too quickly to report a player accurately, run to a quiet spot and review the chat at your leisure!

lines.speaker: Town crier
lines.text: Take time to check the second trade window carefully. Don't be scammed!

lines.speaker: Town crier
lines.text: Did you know having a bank pin can help secure your valuable items?

lines.speaker: Town crier
lines.text: Did you know that mithril equipment is very light?

lines.speaker: Town crier
lines.text: There are no cheats in RuneScape! Never visit websites promising otherwise!

lines.speaker: Town crier
lines.text: Jagex will never e-mail you. Do not believe any e-mails that claim to be from us.

lines.speaker: Town crier
lines.text: If you're stuck on a skill, talk to the Lumbridge Tutors for help and tips.

lines.speaker: Town crier
lines.text: Did you know that you can wear a shield with a crossbow?

lines.speaker: Town crier
lines.text: Beware of players trying to lure you into the wilderness. Your items cannot be returned if you lose them!

lines.speaker: Town crier
lines.text: If a player isn't sure of the rules, send them to me! I'll be happy to remind them!

lines.speaker: Town crier
lines.text: Never tell your password to anyone, not even your best friend!

lines.speaker: Town crier
lines.text: Be careful when fighting wizards! Wearing heavy armour can lower your Magic resistance!

lines.speaker: Town crier
lines.text: Is there anything else you'd like to know?

lines.speaker: Town crier
lines.text: Hear ye! Hear ye! Player Moderators massive help to Rune... Oh great, a P-mod! I know all about the great job you guys do, would you like me to prove my extensive knowledge to you?!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you show me the Guidelines please?

lines.speaker: Town crier
lines.text: Of course, but I can't let you keep it - I've only got a few copies!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you have a copy of the Code of Conduct?

lines.speaker: Town crier
lines.text: Oh yes, but I can't let you keep it - I've only a few copies!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you give me a moderating tip please?

lines.speaker: Town crier
lines.text: Add other Player Moderators to your friends list for quick advice and moral support in-game. It does help!

lines.speaker: Town crier
lines.text: To avoid reporting the wrong player, add an offender to your friends list, switch your public chat to friends and then use your right-click report from there. Easy!

lines.speaker: Town crier
lines.text: Did you know that Free-to-Play P-mods CAN visit the moderator forums, by logging into the regular forums at the top right hand side of the forum screen, despite it saying Members only.

lines.speaker: Town crier
lines.text: Don't be tempted to report anything you didn't see, no matter how much you trust the player who is informing you.

lines.speaker: Town crier
lines.text: Don't eat something messy when on a first date - it won't look good!

lines.speaker: Town crier
lines.text: Don't forget to add the mute if needed, before selecting the report category. This cuts down on those duplicate reports for one player.

lines.speaker: Town crier
lines.text: Feeling harassed? Don't forget your ignore list can be especially useful if a player seems to be picking on you!

lines.speaker: Town crier
lines.text: The forums are the best place to keep abreast of any procedure changes that might not yet be included in the Player Mod guidelines!

lines.speaker: Town crier
lines.text: Got players asking you to unban them or help them retrieve a stolen or hacked account? There's nothing you can do so just send them to the Customer Support Team. If they think you can help, send them to me; I'll

lines.speaker: Town crier
lines.text: put them straight!

lines.speaker: Town crier
lines.text: Have the P-Mod Oracle open in a separate window when playing Old School RuneScape - that way you'll always have the most up-to-date guidelines to hand!

lines.speaker: Town crier
lines.text: If the chat is scrolling too fast for you to see that suspect line you want to double check, just run up a ladder or to a quieter spot to stop the chat screen scrolling. Then you can scroll back and read that

lines.speaker: Town crier
lines.text: suspect line and judge the situation in your own time!

lines.speaker: Town crier
lines.text: If the chat window is moving too quickly to report a player accurately, run to a quiet spot and review the chat at your leisure!

lines.speaker: Town crier
lines.text: If a player isn't sure of the rules, send them to me! I'll be happy to remind them!

lines.speaker: Town crier
lines.text: If you're a new mod, don't forget to read the guidelines before you log into the game! If you've been a P-mod for a while, why not have another read of them to refresh your memory and to get up to date

lines.speaker: Town crier
lines.text: with any changes you may have missed!

lines.speaker: Town crier
lines.text: If you've got a player asking you questions about P- mods, feel free to send them to me; I can answer questions about P-mods for you!

lines.speaker: Town crier
lines.text: If you get harassed by a player in game, don't take it personally - the player is reacting to your silver crown, not to you!

lines.speaker: Town crier
lines.text: If you notice a player trying to buy something common for an extremely high price, look about and see if there's someone else selling the same item for a much lower price. Chances are they're team scamming. If so,

lines.speaker: Town crier
lines.text: write their names down and send your J-mod curators a query!

lines.speaker: Town crier
lines.text: If you see a player abusing a bug in game, there's no need to report the player, just submit a bug report via the in-game report form.

lines.speaker: Town crier
lines.text: An important part of being a P-mod is to act as an ambassador for the game. Keeping your cool even when faced with the rudest players goes a long way towards this!

lines.speaker: Town crier
lines.text: It's always better to report in secret. Telling a player that you are about to report them rarely makes you a friend, and can often lead to a player being offensive to you!

lines.speaker: Town crier
lines.text: Make sure you log out of the game and the forums whenever you leave your device. Don't allow others to see confidential info!

lines.speaker: Town crier
lines.text: Never answer questions if you feel the answer will breach confidentiality.

lines.speaker: Town crier
lines.text: The P-mod forums are your friend! They're the best place to talk to and meet other P-mods and are absolutely packed with useful advice!

lines.speaker: Town crier
lines.text: Remember, if in doubt, report without a mute! It's very frustrating not to be able to speak if you're innocent!

lines.speaker: Town crier
lines.text: Remember, if offensive language has been completely filtered out then there's no need to report or mute it!

lines.speaker: Town crier
lines.text: Think twice before applying that mute. Will it really prevent further damage from surrounding players?

lines.speaker: Town crier
lines.text: Use the Jagex PMod team if you need to - they're there to help!

lines.speaker: Town crier
lines.text: You'll be amazed how often a bit of humour can diffuse a potentially unpleasant situation, before it becomes too serious.

lines.speaker: Town crier
lines.text: Your J-mods are there to help you. If you ever need their advice, send them a query. They are always very happy to help. Don't be afraid to use them!

lines.speaker: Town crier
lines.text: Your Player Moderator identity must never be revealed on the regular Old School RuneScape forums. However, you're more than welcome to chat about all aspects of being a Player Moderator in the official

lines.speaker: Town crier
lines.text: Player Moderator forums!

lines.speaker: Town crier
lines.text: Is there anything else you'd like to know?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No thanks.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You're very helpful to us Player Moderators, but what help do you give to non Player Moderators?

lines.speaker: Town crier
lines.text: Well now, let me see...

lines.speaker: Town Crier
lines.text: Make sure your account security is up to date! (Date unknown)

lines.speaker: Town Crier
lines.text: Sailing has been announced! Coming November 19th! (2 Nov 2025)

lines.speaker: Town Crier
lines.text: Are you ready for Sailing on November 19th?! (2 Nov 2025)

lines.speaker: Town Crier
lines.text: Have you checked out the latest additions to Varlamore yet? (13 Aug 2025 – 20 Aug 2025)

lines.speaker: Town Crier
lines.text: Thanks for helping us break player records! (20 Aug 2025 – 27 Aug 2025)

lines.speaker: Town Crier
lines.text: The Summer Sweep-Up Slayer & Misc changes are here! (27 Aug 2025 – 03 Sep 2025)

lines.speaker: Town Crier
lines.text: The Combat Achievements for the Doom of Mokhaiotl are here! (03 Sep 2025 – 17 Sep 2025)

lines.speaker: Town Crier
lines.text: Not long now until Grid Master! Are you up to date on all the latest infomation? (17 Sep 2025 – 16 Oct 2025)

lines.speaker: Town Crier
lines.text: Old School has loads going on right now! Keep up to date with our latest newsposts! (Date unknown – 16 Oct 2025)

lines.speaker: Town Crier
lines.text: Grid Master has arrived! Time to get your game on! (16 Oct 2025 - 2 Nov 2025)

lines.speaker: Town Crier
lines.text: It's spooky season! Halloween is here! (16 Oct 2025 - 2 Nov 2025)
